questions.question: What is the default scope for a Spring bean annotated with @Service?
options: prototype — a new instance per injection
request — a new instance per HTTP request
singleton — one instance per Spring application context
session — one instance per user session

questions.question: A ShoppingCart bean is session-scoped. Why does injecting it into a singleton OrderService cause a problem WITHOUT proxyMode?
options: Session beans are immutable and cannot be assigned to singleton fields
At application startup, no HTTP session exists — Spring cannot create the session bean to inject
Singleton beans cannot depend on any non-singleton beans
Session beans require HTTP Basic Auth to be injected

questions.question: What is the difference between @PostConstruct and a constructor for initialization logic?
options: They are identical — both run after all dependencies are injected
Constructor runs before dependency injection completes; @PostConstruct runs after all @Autowired fields are set
@PostConstruct only works with @Service beans; constructors work with any class
Constructor is faster; @PostConstruct has a startup delay

questions.question: Which scope should be used for a stateless service like UserService that calculates logic and delegates to a repository?
options: prototype — ensures no shared state between callers
request — tied to HTTP request lifecycle
singleton — stateless, safe to share across all requests
application — lives for the application lifetime but separate from singleton

questions.question: When is @PreDestroy called?
options: Before each method call on the bean
When the bean is garbage collected by the JVM
When the Spring application context is shutting down (e.g., application stop)
After every HTTP request when using request-scoped beans
